title: pinus clusiana subsp. mauretanica
intro: Pinus clusiana subsp. mauretanica, also known as the Moroccan pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of Morocco. The resin of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems, thanks to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The resin can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infused oils, which are used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
